NKJV.1: Dishonest scales are an abomination to the LORD, But a just weight is His delight.
NKJV.2: When pride comes, then comes shame; But with the humble is wisdom.
NKJV.3: The integrity of the upright will guide them, But the perversity of the unfaithful will destroy them.
NKJV.4: Riches do not profit in the day of wrath, But righteousness delivers from death.
NKJV.5: The righteousness of the blameless will direct his way aright, But the wicked will fall by his own wickedness.
NKJV.6: The righteousness of the upright will deliver them, But the unfaithful will be caught by their lust.
NKJV.7: When a wicked man dies, his expectation will perish, And the hope of the unjust perishes.
NKJV.8: The righteous is delivered from trouble, And it comes to the wicked instead.
NKJV.9: The hypocrite with his mouth destroys his neighbor, But through knowledge the righteous will be delivered.
NKJV.10: When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices; And when the wicked perish, there is jubilation.
NKJV.11: By the blessing of the upright the city is exalted, But it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.
NKJV.12: He who is devoid of wisdom despises his neighbor, But a man of understanding holds his peace.
NKJV.13: A talebearer reveals secrets, But he who is of a faithful spirit conceals a matter.
NKJV.14: Where there is no counsel, the people fall; But in the multitude of counselors there is safety.
NKJV.15: He who is surety for a stranger will suffer, But one who hates being surety is secure.
NKJV.16: A gracious woman retains honor, But ruthless men retain riches.
NKJV.17: The merciful man does good for his own soul, But he who is cruel troubles his own flesh.
NKJV.18: The wicked man does deceptive work, But he who sows righteousness will have a sure reward.
NKJV.19: As righteousness leads to life, So he who pursues evil pursues it to his own death.
NKJV.20: Those who are of a perverse heart are an abomination to the LORD, But the blameless in their ways are His delight.
NKJV.21: Though they join forces, the wicked will not go unpunished; But the posterity of the righteous will be delivered.
NKJV.22: As a ring of gold in a swine’s snout, So is a lovely woman who lacks discretion.
NKJV.23: The desire of the righteous is only good, But the expectation of the wicked is wrath.
NKJV.24: There is one who scatters, yet increases more; And there is one who withholds more than is right, But it leads to poverty.
NKJV.25: The generous soul will be made rich,  And he who waters will also be watered himself.
NKJV.26: The people will curse him who withholds grain, But blessing will be on the head of him who sells it.
NKJV.27: He who earnestly seeks good finds favor, But trouble will come to him who seeks evil.
NKJV.28: He who trusts in his riches will fall, But the righteous will flourish like foliage.
NKJV.29: He who troubles his own house will inherit the wind, And the fool will be servant to the wise of heart.
NKJV.30: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, And he who wins souls is wise.
NKJV.31: If the righteous will be recompensed on the earth, How much more the ungodly and the sinner.

ESV.1: A false balance is an abomination to the LORD, but a just weight is his delight.
ESV.2: When pride comes, then comes disgrace, but with the humble is wisdom.
ESV.3: The integrity of the upright guides them, but the crookedness of the treacherous destroys them.
ESV.4: Riches do not profit in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death.
ESV.5: The righteousness of the blameless keeps his way straight, but the wicked falls by his own wickedness.
ESV.6: The righteousness of the upright delivers them, but the treacherous are taken captive by their lust.
ESV.7: When the wicked dies, his hope will perish, and the expectation of wealth  perishes too.
ESV.8: The righteous is delivered from trouble, and the wicked walks into it instead.
ESV.9: With his mouth the godless man would destroy his neighbor, but by knowledge the righteous are delivered.
ESV.10: When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices, and when the wicked perish there are shouts of gladness.
ESV.11: By the blessing of the upright a city is exalted, but by the mouth of the wicked it is overthrown.
ESV.12: Whoever belittles his neighbor lacks sense, but a man of understanding remains silent.
ESV.13: Whoever goes about slandering reveals secrets, but he who is trustworthy in spirit keeps a thing covered.
ESV.14: Where there is no guidance, a people falls, but in an abundance of counselors there is safety.
ESV.15: Whoever puts up security for a stranger will surely suffer harm, but he who hates striking hands in pledge is secure.
ESV.16: A gracious woman gets honor, and violent men get riches.
ESV.17: A man who is kind benefits himself, but a cruel man hurts himself.
ESV.18: The wicked earns deceptive wages, but one who sows righteousness gets a sure reward.
ESV.19: Whoever is steadfast in righteousness will live, but he who pursues evil will die.
ESV.20: Those of crooked heart are an abomination to the LORD, but those of blameless ways are his delight.
ESV.21: Be assured, an evil person will not go unpunished, but the offspring of the righteous will be delivered.
ESV.22: Like a gold ring in a pig's snout is a beautiful woman without discretion.
ESV.23: The desire of the righteous ends only in good, the expectation of the wicked in wrath.
ESV.24: One gives freely, yet grows all the richer; another withholds what he should give, and only suffers want.
ESV.25: Whoever brings blessing will be enriched, and one who waters will himself be watered.
ESV.26: The people curse him who holds back grain, but a blessing is on the head of him who sells it.
ESV.27: Whoever diligently seeks good seeks favor,  but evil comes to him who searches for it.
ESV.28: Whoever trusts in his riches will fall, but the righteous will flourish like a green leaf.
ESV.29: Whoever troubles his own household will inherit the wind, and the fool will be servant to the wise of heart.
ESV.30: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, and whoever captures souls is wise.
ESV.31: If the righteous is repaid on earth, how much more the wicked and the sinner!

NASB.1: A false balance is an abomination to the LORD, But a just weight is His delight.
NASB.2: When pride comes, then comes dishonor, But with the humble is wisdom.
NASB.3: The integrity of the upright will guide them, But the crookedness of the treacherous will destroy them.
NASB.4: Riches do not profit in the day of wrath, But righteousness delivers from death.
NASB.5: The righteousness of the blameless will smooth his way, But the wicked will fall by his own wickedness.
NASB.6: The righteousness of the upright will deliver them, But the treacherous will be caught by [their own] greed.
NASB.7: When a wicked man dies, [his] expectation will perish, And the hope of strong men perishes.
NASB.8: The righteous is delivered from trouble, But the wicked takes his place.
NASB.9: With [his] mouth the godless man destroys his neighbor, But through knowledge the righteous will be delivered.
NASB.10: When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices, And when the wicked perish, there is joyful shouting.
NASB.11: By the blessing of the upright a city is exalted, But by the mouth of the wicked it is torn down.
NASB.12: He who despises his neighbor lacks sense, But a man of understanding keeps silent.
NASB.13: He who goes about as a talebearer reveals secrets, But he who is trustworthy conceals a matter.
NASB.14: Where there is no guidance the people fall, But in abundance of counselors there is victory.
NASB.15: He who is guarantor for a stranger will surely suffer for it, But he who hates being a guarantor is secure.
NASB.16: A gracious woman attains honor, And ruthless men attain riches.
NASB.17: The merciful man does himself good, But the cruel man does himself harm.
NASB.18: The wicked earns deceptive wages, But he who sows righteousness [gets] a true reward.
NASB.19: He who is steadfast in righteousness [will attain] to life, And he who pursues evil [will bring about] his own death.
NASB.20: The perverse in heart are an abomination to the LORD, But the blameless in [their] walk are His delight.
NASB.21: Assuredly, the evil man will not go unpunished, But the descendants of the righteous will be delivered.
NASB.22: [As] a ring of gold in a swine's snout [So is] a beautiful woman who lacks discretion.
NASB.23: The desire of the righteous is only good, [But] the expectation of the wicked is wrath.
NASB.24: There is one who scatters, and [yet] increases all the more, And there is one who withholds what is justly due, [and yet it results] only in want.
NASB.25: The generous man will be prosperous, And he who waters will himself be watered.
NASB.26: He who withholds grain, the people will curse him, But blessing will be on the head of him who sells [it].
NASB.27: He who diligently seeks good seeks favor, But he who seeks evil, evil will come to him.
NASB.28: He who trusts in his riches will fall, But the righteous will flourish like the [green] leaf.
NASB.29: He who troubles his own house will inherit wind, And the foolish will be servant to the wisehearted.
NASB.30: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, And he who is wise wins souls.
NASB.31: If the righteous will be rewarded in the earth, How much more the wicked and the sinner!

NLT.1:
The LORD detests the use of dishonest scales,
but he delights in accurate weights.
NLT.2:
Pride leads to disgrace,
but with humility comes wisdom.
NLT.3:
Honesty guides good people;
dishonesty destroys treacherous people.
NLT.4:
Riches won’t help on the day of judgment,
but right living can save you from death.
NLT.5:
The godly are directed by honesty;
the wicked fall beneath their load of sin.
NLT.6:
The godliness of good people rescues them;
the ambition of treacherous people traps them.
NLT.7:
When the wicked die, their hopes die with them,
for they rely on their own feeble strength.
NLT.8:
The godly are rescued from trouble,
and it falls on the wicked instead.
NLT.9:
With their words, the godless destroy their friends,
but knowledge will rescue the righteous.
NLT.10:
The whole city celebrates when the godly succeed;
they shout for joy when the wicked die.
NLT.11:
Upright citizens are good for a city and make it prosper,
but the talk of the wicked tears it apart.
NLT.12:
It is foolish to belittle one’s neighbor;
a sensible person keeps quiet.
NLT.13:
A gossip goes around telling secrets,
but those who are trustworthy can keep a confidence.
NLT.14:
Without wise leadership, a nation falls;
there is safety in having many advisers.
NLT.15:
There’s danger in putting up security for a stranger’s debt;
it’s safer not to guarantee another person’s debt.
NLT.16:
A gracious woman gains respect,
but ruthless men gain only wealth.
NLT.17:
Your kindness will reward you,
but your cruelty will destroy you.
NLT.18:
Evil people get rich for the moment,
but the reward of the godly will last.
NLT.19:
Godly people find life;
evil people find death.
NLT.20:
The LORD detests people with crooked hearts,
but he delights in those with integrity.
NLT.21:
Evil people will surely be punished,
but the children of the godly will go free.
NLT.22:
A beautiful woman who lacks discretion
is like a gold ring in a pig’s snout.
NLT.23:
The godly can look forward to a reward,
while the wicked can expect only judgment.
NLT.24:
Give freely and become more wealthy;
be stingy and lose everything.
NLT.25:
The generous will prosper;
those who refresh others will themselves be refreshed.
NLT.26:
People curse those who hoard their grain,
but they bless the one who sells in time of need.
NLT.27:
If you search for good, you will find favor;
but if you search for evil, it will find you!
NLT.28:
Trust in your money and down you go!
But the godly flourish like leaves in spring.
NLT.29:
Those who bring trouble on their families inherit the wind.
The fool will be a servant to the wise.
NLT.30:
The seeds of good deeds become a tree of life;
a wise person wins friends.
NLT.31:
If the righteous are rewarded here on earth,
what will happen to wicked sinners?

WEB.1: A false balance is an abomination to Yahweh, but accurate weights are his delight.
WEB.2: When pride comes, then comes shame, but with humility comes wisdom.
WEB.3: The integrity of the upright shall guide them, but the perverseness of the treacherous shall destroy them.
WEB.4: Riches don’t profit in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death.
WEB.5: The righteousness of the blameless will direct his way, but the wicked shall fall by his own wickedness.
WEB.6: The righteousness of the upright shall deliver them, but the unfaithful will be trapped by evil desires.
WEB.7: When a wicked man dies, hope perishes, and expectation of power comes to nothing.
WEB.8: A righteous person is delivered out of trouble, and the wicked takes his place.
WEB.9: With his mouth the godless man destroys his neighbor, but the righteous will be delivered through knowledge.
WEB.10: When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices. When the wicked perish, there is shouting.
WEB.11: By the blessing of the upright, the city is exalted, but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.
WEB.12: One who despises his neighbor is void of wisdom, but a man of understanding holds his peace.
WEB.13: One who brings gossip betrays a confidence, but one who is of a trustworthy spirit is one who keeps a secret.
WEB.14: Where there is no wise guidance, the nation falls, but in the multitude of counselors there is victory.
WEB.15: He who is collateral for a stranger will suffer for it, but he who refuses pledges of collateral is secure.
WEB.16: A gracious woman obtains honor, but violent men obtain riches.
WEB.17: The merciful man does good to his own soul, but he who is cruel troubles his own flesh.
WEB.18: Wicked people earn deceitful wages, but one who sows righteousness reaps a sure reward.
WEB.19: He who is truly righteous gets life. He who pursues evil gets death.
WEB.20: Those who are perverse in heart are an abomination to Yahweh, but those whose ways are blameless are his delight.
WEB.21: Most certainly, the evil man will not be unpunished, but the offspring  of the righteous will be delivered.
WEB.22: Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout, is a beautiful woman who lacks discretion.
WEB.23: The desire of the righteous is only good. The expectation of the wicked is wrath.
WEB.24: There is one who scatters, and increases yet more. There is one who withholds more than is appropriate, but gains poverty.
WEB.25: The liberal soul shall be made fat. He who waters shall be watered also himself.
WEB.26: People curse someone who withholds grain, but blessing will be on the head of him who sells it.
WEB.27: He who diligently seeks good seeks favor, but he who searches after evil, it shall come to him.
WEB.28: He who trusts in his riches will fall, but the righteous shall flourish as the green leaf.
WEB.29: He who troubles his own house shall inherit the wind. The foolish shall be servant to the wise of heart.
WEB.30: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life. He who is wise wins souls.
WEB.31: Behold, the righteous shall be repaid in the earth; how much more the wicked and the sinner!

CSB17.1:
Dishonest scales are detestable to the LORD, ⓛ
but an accurate weight is his delight. ⓜ
CSB17.2:
When arrogance comes, disgrace follows,
but with humility comes wisdom. ⓝ
CSB17.3:
The integrity of the upright guides them, ⓐ

but righteousness rescues from death. ⓒ
CSB17.5:
The righteousness of the blameless clears his path,
but the wicked person will fall because of his wickedness.
CSB17.6:
The righteousness of the upright rescues them,
but the treacherous are trapped by their own desires. ⓓ
CSB17.7:
When the wicked person dies,
his expectation comes to nothing,
and hope placed in wealth [1] [2] vanishes. ⓔ
CSB17.8:
The righteous one is rescued from trouble;
in his place, the wicked one goes in. ⓕ
CSB17.9:
With his mouth the ungodly destroys his neighbor,
but through knowledge the righteous are rescued.
CSB17.10:
When the righteous thrive, a city rejoices; ⓖ
when the wicked die, there is joyful shouting.
CSB17.11:
A city is built up by the blessing of the upright, ⓗ
but it is torn down by the mouth of the wicked. ⓘ
CSB17.12:
Whoever shows contempt for his neighbor lacks sense,
but a person with understanding keeps silent. ⓙ
CSB17.13:
A gossip goes around revealing a secret,
but a trustworthy person keeps a confidence. ⓚ
CSB17.14:
Without guidance, a people will fall,
but with many counselors there is deliverance. ⓛ
CSB17.15:
If someone puts up security for a stranger,
he will suffer for it, ⓜ
but the one who hates such agreements is protected.
CSB17.16:
A gracious woman gains honor,
but violent [3] people gain only riches.
CSB17.17:
A kind man benefits himself, ⓝ
but a cruel person brings ruin on himself. ⓐ
CSB17.18:

but the one who sows righteousness, a true reward. ⓑ
CSB17.19:
Genuine righteousness leads to life, ⓒ
but pursuing evil leads to death. ⓓ
CSB17.20:
Those with twisted minds are detestable to the LORD,
but those with blameless conduct are his delight. ⓔ
CSB17.21:
Be assured [4] that a wicked person
will not go unpunished, ⓕ
but the offspring of the righteous will escape.
CSB17.22:
A beautiful woman who rejects good sense
is like a gold ring in a pig’s snout. ⓖ
CSB17.23:
The desire of the righteous turns out well, ⓗ
but the hope of the wicked leads to wrath. ⓘ
CSB17.24:
One person gives freely,
yet gains more; ⓙ
another withholds what is right,
only to become poor. ⓚ
CSB17.25:
A generous person will be enriched, ⓛ
and the one who gives a drink of water
will receive water. ⓜ
CSB17.26:
People will curse ⓝ anyone who hoards grain,
but a blessing will come to the one who sells it. ⓐ
CSB17.27:
The one who searches for what is good ⓑ seeks favor,
but if someone looks for trouble, it will come to him. ⓒ
CSB17.28:
Anyone trusting in his riches will fall, ⓓ
but the righteous will flourish like foliage. ⓔ
CSB17.29:
The one who brings ruin on his household ⓕ
will inherit the wind, ⓖ
and a fool will be a slave
to someone whose heart is wise.
CSB17.30:
The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, ⓗ
and a wise person captivates people.
CSB17.31:
If the righteous will be repaid on earth,
how much more the wicked and sinful. ⓘ

AMP.1:
A false balance and dishonest business practices are extremely offensive to the LORD,
But an accurate scale is His delight.
AMP.2:
When pride comes [boiling up with an arrogant attitude of self-importance], then come dishonor and shame,
But with the humble [the teachable who have been chiseled by trial and who have learned to walk humbly with God] there is wisdom and soundness of mind.
AMP.3:
The integrity and moral courage of the upright will guide them,
But the crookedness of the treacherous will destroy them.
AMP.4:
Riches will not provide security in the day of wrath and judgment,
But righteousness rescues from death.
AMP.5:
The righteousness of the blameless will smooth their way and keep it straight,
But the wicked will fall by his own wickedness.
AMP.6:
The righteousness of the upright will rescue them,
But the treacherous will be caught by their own greed.
AMP.7:
When the wicked man dies, his expectation will perish;
And the hope of [godless] strong men perishes.
AMP.8:
The righteous is rescued from trouble,
And the wicked takes his place.
AMP.9:
With his mouth the godless man destroys his neighbor,
But through knowledge and discernment the righteous will be rescued.
AMP.10:
When it goes well for the righteous, the city rejoices,
And when the wicked perish, there are shouts of joy.
AMP.11:
By the blessing [of the influence] of the upright the city is exalted,
But by the mouth of the wicked it is torn down.
AMP.12:
He who despises his neighbor lacks sense,
But a man of understanding keeps silent.
AMP.13:
He who goes about as a gossip reveals secrets,
But he who is trustworthy and faithful keeps a matter hidden.
AMP.14:
Where there is no [wise, intelligent] guidance, the people fall [and go off course like a ship without a helm],
But in the abundance of [wise and godly] counselors there is victory.
AMP.15:
He who puts up security and guarantees a debt for an outsider will surely suffer [for his foolishness],
But he who hates (declines) being a guarantor is secure [from its penalties].
AMP.16:
A gracious and good woman attains honor,
And ruthless men attain riches [but not respect].
AMP.17:
The merciful and generous man benefits his soul [for his behavior returns to bless him],
But the cruel and callous man does himself harm.
AMP.18:
The wicked man earns deceptive wages,
But he who sows righteousness and lives his life with integrity will have a true reward [that is both permanent and satisfying].
AMP.19:
He who is steadfast in righteousness attains life,
But he who pursues evil attains his own death.
AMP.20:
The perverse in heart are repulsive and shamefully vile to the LORD,
But those who are blameless and above reproach in their walk are His delight!
AMP.21:
Assuredly, the evil man will not go unpunished,
But the descendants of the righteous will be freed.
AMP.22:
As a ring of gold in a swine's snout,
So is a beautiful woman who is without discretion [her lack of character mocks her beauty].
AMP.23:
The desire of the righteous brings only good,
But the expectation of the wicked brings wrath.
AMP.24:
There is the one who [generously] scatters [abroad], and yet increases all the more;
And there is the one who withholds what is justly due, but it results only in want and poverty.
AMP.25:
The generous man [is a source of blessing and] shall be prosperous and enriched,
And he who waters will himself be watered [reaping the generosity he has sown].
AMP.26:
The people curse him who holds back grain [when the public needs it],
But a blessing [from God and man] is upon the head of him who sells it.
AMP.27:
He who diligently seeks good seeks favor and grace,
But he who seeks evil, evil will come to him.
AMP.28:
He who leans on and trusts in and is confident in his riches will fall,
But the righteous [who trust in God's provision] will flourish like a green leaf.
AMP.29:
He who troubles (mismanages) his own house will inherit the wind (nothing),
And the foolish will be a servant to the wise-hearted.
AMP.30:
The fruit of the [consistently] righteous is a tree of life,
And he who is wise captures and wins souls [for God—he gathers them for eternity].
AMP.31:
If the righteous will be rewarded on the earth [with godly blessings],
How much more [will] the wicked and the sinner [be repaid with punishment]!

MSG.1:
God hates cheating in the marketplace;
he loves it when business is aboveboard.
MSG.2:
The stuck-up fall flat on their faces,
but down-to-earth people stand firm.
MSG.3:
The integrity of the honest keeps them on track;
the deviousness of crooks brings them to ruin.
MSG.4:
A thick bankroll is no help when life falls apart,
but a principled life can stand up to the worst.
MSG.5:
Moral character makes for smooth traveling;
an evil life is a hard life.
MSG.6:
Good character is the best insurance;
crooks get trapped in their sinful lust.
MSG.7:
When the wicked die, that's it—
the story's over, end of hope.
MSG.8:
A good person is saved from much trouble;
a bad person runs straight into it.
MSG.9:
The loose tongue of the godless spreads destruction;
the common sense of the godly preserves them.
MSG.10:
When it goes well for good people, the whole town cheers;
when it goes badly for bad people, the town celebrates.
MSG.11:
When right-living people bless the city, it flourishes;
evil talk turns it into a ghost town in no time.
MSG.12:
Mean-spirited slander is heartless;
quiet discretion accompanies good sense.
MSG.13:
A gadabout gossip can't be trusted with a secret,
but someone of integrity won't violate a confidence.
MSG.14:
Without good direction, people lose their way;
the more wise counsel you follow, the better your chances.
MSG.15:
Whoever makes deals with strangers is sure to get burned;
if you keep a cool head, you'll avoid rash bargains.
MSG.16:
A woman of gentle grace gets respect,
but men of rough violence grab for loot.
MSG.17:
When you're kind to others, you help yourself;
when you're cruel to others, you hurt yourself.
MSG.18:
Bad work gets paid with a bad check;
good work gets solid pay.
MSG.19:
Take your stand with God's loyal community and live,
or chase after phantoms of evil and die.
MSG.20:
God can't stand deceivers,
but oh how he relishes integrity.
MSG.21:
Count on this: The wicked won't get off scot-free,
and God's loyal people will triumph.
MSG.22:
Like a gold ring in a pig's snout
is a beautiful face on an empty head.
MSG.23:
The desires of good people lead straight to the best,
but wicked ambition ends in angry frustration.
MSG.24:
The world of the generous gets larger and larger;
the world of the stingy gets smaller and smaller.
MSG.25:
The one who blesses others is abundantly blessed;
those who help others are helped.
MSG.26:
Curses on those who drive a hard bargain!
Blessings on all who play fair and square!
MSG.27:
The one who seeks good finds delight;
the student of evil becomes evil.
MSG.28:
A life devoted to things is a dead life, a stump;
a God-shaped life is a flourishing tree.
MSG.29:
Exploit or abuse your family, and end up with a fistful of air;
common sense tells you it's a stupid way to live.
MSG.30:
A good life is a fruit-bearing tree;
a violent life destroys souls.
MSG.31:
If good people barely make it,
what's in store for the bad!

NIV.1:
The Lord abhors dishonest scales,
but accurate weights are his delight.
NIV.2:
When pride comes, then comes disgrace,
but with humility comes wisdom.
NIV.3:
The integrity of the upright guides them,
but the unfaithful are destroyed by their duplicity.
NIV.4:
Wealth is worthless in the day of wrath,
but righteousness delivers from death.
NIV.5:
The righteousness of the blameless makes a straight way for them,
but the wicked are brought down by their own wickedness.
NIV.6:
The righteousness of the upright delivers them,
but the unfaithful are trapped by evil desires.
NIV.7:
When a wicked man dies, his hope perishes;
all he expected from his power comes to nothing.
NIV.8:
The righteous man is rescued from trouble,
and it comes on the wicked instead.
NIV.9:
With his mouth the godless destroys his neighbor,
but through knowledge the righteous escape.
NIV.10:
When the righteous prosper, the city rejoices;
when the wicked perish, there are shouts of joy.
NIV.11:
Through the blessing of the upright a city is exalted,
but by the mouth of the wicked it is destroyed.
NIV.12:
A man who lacks judgment derides his neighbor,
but a man of understanding holds his tongue.
NIV.13:
A gossip betrays a confidence,
but a trustworthy man keeps a secret.
NIV.14:
For lack of guidance a nation falls,
but many advisers make victory sure.
NIV.15:
He who puts up security for another will surely suffer,
but whoever refuses to strike hands in pledge is safe.
NIV.16:
A kindhearted woman gains respect,
but ruthless men gain only wealth.
NIV.17:
A kind man benefits himself,
but a cruel man brings trouble on himself.
NIV.18:
The wicked man earns deceptive wages,
but he who sows righteousness reaps a sure reward.
NIV.19:
The truly righteous man attains life,
but he who pursues evil goes to his death.
NIV.20:
The Lord detests men of perverse heart
but he delights in those whose ways are blameless.
NIV.21:
Be sure of this: The wicked will not go unpunished,
but those who are righteous will go free.
NIV.22:
Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout
is a beautiful woman who shows no discretion.
NIV.23:
The desire of the righteous ends only in good,
but the hope of the wicked only in wrath.
NIV.24:
One man gives freely, yet gains even more;
another withholds unduly, but comes to poverty.
NIV.25:
A generous man will prosper;
he who refreshes others will himself be refreshed.
NIV.26:
People curse the man who hoards grain,
but blessing crowns him who is willing to sell.
NIV.27:
He who seeks good finds goodwill,
but evil comes to him who searches for it.
NIV.28:
Whoever trusts in his riches will fall,
but the righteous will thrive like a green leaf.
NIV.29:
He who brings trouble on his family will inherit only wind,
and the fool will be servant to the wise.
NIV.30:
The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life,
and he who wins souls is wise.
NIV.31:
If the righteous receive their due on earth,
how much more the ungodly and the sinner!

NRSVCE.1:
A false balance is an abomination to the LORD,
but an accurate weight is his delight.
NRSVCE.2:
When pride comes, then comes disgrace;
but wisdom is with the humble.
NRSVCE.3:
The integrity of the upright guides them,
but the crookedness of the treacherous destroys them.
NRSVCE.4:
Riches do not profit in the day of wrath,
but righteousness delivers from death.
NRSVCE.5:
The righteousness of the blameless keeps their ways straight,
but the wicked fall by their own wickedness.
NRSVCE.6:
The righteousness of the upright saves them,
but the treacherous are taken captive by their schemes.
NRSVCE.7:
When the wicked die, their hope perishes,
and the expectation of the godless comes to nothing.
NRSVCE.8:
The righteous are delivered from trouble,
and the wicked get into it instead.
NRSVCE.9:
With their mouths the godless would destroy their neighbors,
but by knowledge the righteous are delivered.
NRSVCE.10:
When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices;
and when the wicked perish, there is jubilation.
NRSVCE.11:
By the blessing of the upright a city is exalted,
but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.
NRSVCE.12:
Whoever belittles another lacks sense,
but an intelligent person remains silent.
NRSVCE.13:
A gossip goes about telling secrets,
but one who is trustworthy in spirit keeps a confidence.
NRSVCE.14:
Where there is no guidance, a nation falls,
but in an abundance of counselors there is safety.
NRSVCE.15:
To guarantee loans for a stranger brings trouble,
but there is safety in refusing to do so.
NRSVCE.16:
A gracious woman gets honor,
but she who hates virtue is covered with shame.
The timid become destitute,
but the aggressive gain riches.
NRSVCE.17:
Those who are kind reward themselves,
but the cruel do themselves harm.
NRSVCE.18:
The wicked earn no real gain,
but those who sow righteousness get a true reward.
NRSVCE.19:
Whoever is steadfast in righteousness will live,
but whoever pursues evil will die.
NRSVCE.20:
Crooked minds are an abomination to the LORD,
but those of blameless ways are his delight.
NRSVCE.21:
Be assured, the wicked will not go unpunished,
but those who are righteous will escape.
NRSVCE.22:
Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout
is a beautiful woman without good sense.
NRSVCE.23:
The desire of the righteous ends only in good;
the expectation of the wicked in wrath.
NRSVCE.24:
Some give freely, yet grow all the richer;
others withhold what is due, and only suffer want.
NRSVCE.25:
A generous person will be enriched,
and one who gives water will get water.
NRSVCE.26:
The people curse those who hold back grain,
but a blessing is on the head of those who sell it.
NRSVCE.27:
Whoever diligently seeks good seeks favor,
but evil comes to the one who searches for it.
NRSVCE.28:
Those who trust in their riches will wither,
but the righteous will flourish like green leaves.
NRSVCE.29:
Those who trouble their households will inherit wind,
and the fool will be servant to the wise.
NRSVCE.30:
The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life,
but violence takes lives away.
NRSVCE.31:
If the righteous are repaid on earth,
how much more the wicked and the sinner!

NET.1: The LORD abhors dishonest scales, but an accurate weight is his delight.
NET.2: When pride comes, then comes disgrace, but with humility comes wisdom.
NET.3: The integrity of the upright guides them, but the crookedness of the unfaithful destroys them.
NET.4: Wealth does not profit in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from mortal danger.
NET.5: The righteousness of the blameless will make straight their way, but the wicked person will fall by his own wickedness.
NET.6: The righteousness of the upright will deliver them, but the faithless will be captured by their own desires.
NET.7: When a wicked person dies, his expectation perishes, and the hope of his strength perishes.
NET.8: The righteous person is delivered out of trouble, and the wicked turns up in his stead.
NET.9: With his speech the godless person destroys his neighbor, but by knowledge the righteous will be delivered.
NET.10: When the righteous do well, the city rejoices; when the wicked perish, there is joy.
NET.11: A city is exalted by the blessing provided from the upright, but it is destroyed by the counsel of the wicked.
NET.12: The one who denounces his neighbor lacks wisdom, but the one who has discernment keeps silent.
NET.13: The one who goes about slandering others reveals secrets, but the one who is trustworthy conceals a matter.
NET.14: When there is no guidance a nation falls, but there is success in the abundance of counselors.
NET.15: The one who puts up security for a stranger will surely have trouble, but whoever avoids shaking hands will be secure.
NET.16: A generous woman gains honor, and ruthless men seize wealth.
NET.17: A kind person benefits himself, but a cruel person brings himself trouble.
NET.18: The wicked person earns deceitful wages, but the one who sows righteousness reaps a genuine reward.
NET.19: True righteousness leads to life, but the one who pursues evil pursues it to his own death.
NET.20: The LORD abhors those who are perverse in heart, but those who are blameless in their ways are his delight.
NET.21: Be assured that the evil person will certainly be punished, but the descendants of the righteous will not suffer unjust judgment.
NET.22: Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout is a beautiful woman who rejects discretion.
NET.23: What the righteous desire leads only to good, but what the wicked hope for leads to wrath.
NET.24: One person is generous and yet grows more wealthy, but another withholds more than he should and comes to poverty.
NET.25: A generous person will be enriched, and the one who provides water for others will himself be satisfied.
NET.26: People will curse the one who withholds grain, but they will praise the one who sells it.
NET.27: The one who diligently seeks good seeks favor, but the one who searches for evil — it will come to him.
NET.28: The one who trusts in his riches will fall, but the righteous will flourish like a green leaf.
NET.29: The one who troubles his family will inherit nothing, and the fool will be a servant to the wise person.
NET.30: The fruit of the righteous is like a tree producing life, and the one who wins souls is wise.
NET.31: If the righteous are recompensed on earth, how much more the wicked sinner!
